title: Simirestis dewildemaniana
intro: Simirestis dewildemaniana, also known as Wildeman's simirestis or Wildeman's Simiri, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Simirestis dewildemaniana include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit significant pharmacological activity. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and decoctions, which are traditionally used in folk medicine in various African countries.
taxonomy: Simirestis dewildemaniana is a genus within the family Stemonuraceae. This family is part of the larger clade of Cornales, a group of flowering plants. The order Cornales is a part of the subclass Asteridae, which is a division within the class Magnoliopsida. Magnoliopsida is one of the two main classes of flowering plants, the other being Liliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is within the larger group called Angiosperm, which is a major division of flowering plants.
